Description:
Take the lead in your own learning. Use your half days to explore topics that matter to you. Focus on activities that support your personal development, build new skills, and enhance your confidence in your role.

Self‑directed learning might include:
Choosing online modules relevant to your role
Reading articles, research, or guidance
Watching recorded webinars
Using half‑day learning time for a personal project
Reflecting on how new learning can improve practice

In today's fast-paced and demanding environments, stress can quickly become overwhelming—especially when support feels limited or out of reach. This session focuses on practical ways to move from stress and pressure toward greater support, resilience, and connection.
Trainer: Health and Wellbeing Team deivered by HALSA Wellbeing

Description: This session aims to raise awareness and looks at some of the main health conditions which may affect men in their lifetime. There is a focus on both physical and mental health with some practical tools to help you stay focused on your health and wellbeing.
Trainer: Health and Wellbeing Team deivered by HALSA Wellbeing

Description: This session focuses on blood pressure and cholesterol and helps you to 'know your numbers'. We demonstrate pratical tips to support your cardiovascular health and promote a healthy lifestyle to manage or improve your health and wellbeing.
Trainer: Health and Wellbeing Team deivered by HALSA Wellbeing

Description: This practical sessions looks at the contributing factors to aches and pains that women may experience during the Menopause. We also look at how to manage these symptoms through self-care with a focus on eating and hydrating well, movement and stress management.
Trainer: Health and Wellbeing Team deivered by HALSA Wellbeing

Description: This session focuses on the importance of YOU and your Health and Wellbeing. We demonstrate how to make self-care habitual by using practical tips to support your overall health and wellbeing. The session covers the 4 key areas of wellbeing with tips on how to: Eat and Hydrate Well, Sleep Well, Think Well and Feel/Move Well.
Trainer: Health and Wellbeing Team deivered by HALSA Wellbeing

Description: This Toolbox Talk teaches you about the importance of Hydration and the link to your Health, Safety and Wellbeing at work. You will leave armed with information about how much to drink, how to do self-checks to ensure you are adequately hydrated and tips on encouraging you to stay focused on hydration.
Trainer: Health and Wellbeing Team deivered by HALSA Wellbeing

Description: In this session you will learn practical tools to create spaces where people feel safe, respected, and heard. We will look at how to foster psychological safety, encourage open dialogue, and navigate challenging or sensitive topics with confidence. Ideal for facilitators, leaders, educators, and anyone guiding group conversations who wants to support inclusive, respectful, and meaningful dialogue.
Trainer: Health and Wellbeing Team deivered by HALSA Wellbeing

Description: During this interactive session staff will learn practical tips on how to promote healthy sleep habits and bring on a state of relaxation easily to aid restorative sleep.
Trainer: Health and Wellbeing Team deivered by HALSA Wellbeing

Description: This session explores the 'Active Bystander' concept, providing practical tools to safely challenge inappropriate behaviors and foster a more inclusive and supportive environment.
An informative session focused on understanding reasonable adjustments in the workplace, ensuring equity for staff with disabilities or long-term health conditions.
This awareness session covers the importance of the Rainbow Badge scheme, LGBTQ+ inclusivity, and how to be an effective ally within the organization.
A deep dive into cultural humility, moving beyond competence to a lifelong commitment to self-evaluation and critique to better support a diverse workforce and community.

Course:
QI Posters: Competition time!
Activity: Create Your Team’s QI Poster for This Year’s Trust Contest
Closing date 01.09.2026
Description:
As part of this year’s Trust‑wide Quality Improvement (QI) contest, each team is asked to create a QI poster showcasing an audit, research project, or improvement initiative that has taken place, or is currently underway, since 1 January 2025.
During your learning half days, you are invited to spend dedicated time working together to start building your poster. The top prize is worth £5,000.

Meet as a group in Teams
Choose the QI project, idea or improvement you want to showcase
Gather key information and data
Agree your main messages about the impact you’ve made
Start creating your draft poster ready for submission

Why this activity is important
This is a chance to:
highlight great work happening in your team
celebrate improvements made for colleagues, patients or services
share learning across the Trust
contribute to a Trust‑wide event

What you need
Your Learning Half Day time

For further information check out February's Trust Matters or the link provided here
See QI poster template using the link here
